rulings.Case Title: PIONEER FINANCIAL, LLC. VS. CHRISHARA G. HOLMES et al
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT CHRISHARA HOLMES Motion To Set Aside Jugment. Grant, no opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Vacate Arbitrator Award Of Attorney'S Fees And Referral To The Cal. Superior Court In Santa Clara County Per The Terms Of The Contested Contract
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, Line 2, RESPONDENT TODD GLASSEY Motion To Vacate Arbitrator Award Of Attorney's Fees And Referral To The Cal. Superior Court In Santa Clara County Per The Terms Of The Contested Contract. Denied. Judgment was entered in this matter in May 2005. The judgment has not been vacated, and therefore, the Court does not have jurisdiction to rule on this motion. Additionally, CCP sec. 1288 provides that a petition to vacate an arbitration award must be served and filed no later than 100 days after the date of the service of a signed copy of the award. The award was served on Respondent on December 2, 2004, and therefore the 100 day time period to file a petition to vacate has expired. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WYNSHIP HILLIER VS. THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Or In The Alternative For The Issuance Of A Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, April 20, 2015, Line 4, Order To Show Cause Or In The Alternative For The Issuance Of A Writ Of Mandate. The court denies the petition for writ of mandate as moot. The declarations of Nancy Sarieh and Jacquie Hale show that respondent has responded to petitioner's public records request. "[I]f respondent has complied with the petitioner's demands after issuance of the alternative writ, the writ has accomplished the purpose of the mandamus proceedings and the petition should be dismissed as moot." Bruce v. Gregory (1967) 65 Cal.2d 666, 671. In addition, respondent is obliged to make a reasonable effort-not an unlimited search-to respond adequately to petitioner's request. See Bertoli v. City of Sebastopol (2015) 233 Cal.App.4th 353, 372. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: KARIN GREIER VS. RUSSELL ROBINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Russell Robinson And Robinson Law Offices To Provide Further Responses To Her Request For Prodution Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday April 20, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KARIN GREIER'S Motion To Compel Defendants Russell Robinson And Robinson Law Offices To Provide Further Responses To Her Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as to RFPD Nos. 6, 11 and 12. The responses to these requests need to indicate that all responsive documents have been produced. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses and produce all responsive documents by May 4, 2015. The motion as to the other RFPD's is denied as the Court finds that the amended responses are sufficient. Defendant shall reimburse Plaintiff the $60 filing fee for this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KARIN GREIER VS. RUSSELL ROBINSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Russell Robinson And Robinson Law Offices To Provide Further Responses To Her Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday April 20, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFF KARIN GREIER'S Motion To Compel Defendant Russell Robinson And Robinson Law Offices To Provide Further Responses To Her Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted as to Interrogatory Nos. 92, 93, 94, 95, 96, 96 [sic], and 97. The Court finds that the responses do not directly answer the interrogatories. Defendant shall provide supplemental responses by May 4, 2015. The motion as to the reminder of the interrogatories is denied as the Court finds that the amended responses are sufficient. Defendant shall reimburse Plaintiff the $60 filing fee for this motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
